$comment: Hand-curated daily-fortune catalog for `pal fortune`. Lighthearted, never predictive. One-line strings under 120 characters, written in the companion's deterministic-fallback voice. Pack authors are welcome to ship their own catalogs.

fortunes.text: Easy start beats clever start. Eat something, then leave camp.
Pals are watching the perimeter. Yours is just to wake up the rest of the way.
Whatever you put off yesterday is the same size today.
Cold morning. Cold mornings reward people who move first.
Drink something hot. The plan can wait two minutes.
If you forget your sphere belt one more time, I'm packing it for you.
First step is the only one you don't already have a plan for. Take it.
Today's weather rewards the patient. Today's weather always does.
Don't push north today. The map'll still be north tomorrow.
Save one revive. You'll know which fight needed it after.
If something looks worth investigating, mark it before you commit. Past-you forgets things.
An alpha at half health is more dangerous than at full. Slow your throws.
When the tree line goes quiet, that's when you check your pals' positions, not the menu.
There's a good ridge somewhere within ten minutes. Go find it before deciding the day's mood.
If you need to retreat, retreat clean. Half-retreats are how parties wipe.
Charcoal first, ingots second, decoration whenever. The order matters more than the speed.
If a pal's been working alone for twenty minutes, swap them out. They notice.
Storage chest sorted today is storage chest still sorted next month.
Your forge runs cleaner when nobody's standing over it. Walk away for a turn.
One repair pass beats three patch passes. Spend the materials.
Empty out the spoiled food. The pals notice that too.
Yesterday's hard fight is today's stories. The pal that almost died is the one with a name now.
You don't have to be productive every session. Sitting at the fire counts.
Whatever you didn't do yesterday is small. Whatever you did is small. Both still moved you forward.
The companion who never speaks isn't the one you keep. Same applies to friends.
Quiet days are not wasted days. They're how the loud days don't break you.
A run that ends with everyone back at base is a complete run. Mileage isn't the metric.
Pals you trained well outlast pals you caught lucky. Both still count.
Tonight's plan can be 'tonight, I rest.' That's a real plan.
